title: bootstrapping
code: library(EmStanS) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300) #> New names: #> New names: #> â€˘ `score` -> `score...1` #> â€˘ `freq` -> `freq...2` #> â€˘ `Grade` -> `Grade...3` #> â€˘ `score` -> `score...4` #> â€˘ `freq` -> `freq...5` #> â€˘ `Grade` -> `Grade...6`    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 Ă— 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> # â„ą 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 Ă— 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # â„ą 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 Ă— 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level1 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level2 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2 #> # â„ą 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 226 Ă— 9 #>   score...1 freq...2 Grade...3 score...4 freq...5 Grade...6 score...7 freq...8 #>       <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> #> 1        86        6 M1              113       10 M2              126        8 #> 2        87        6 M1              114        2 M2              127        7 #> 3        88        2 M1              115        3 M2              128        5 #> 4        89        4 M1              116        4 M2              129        5 #> 5        90        6 M1              117        7 M2              130        6 #> # â„ą 221 more rows #> # â„ą 1 more variable: Grade...9 <chr>  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                tests = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

title: getting-started
code: library(EmStanS) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300) #> New names: #> New names: #> â€˘ `score` -> `score...1` #> â€˘ `freq` -> `freq...2` #> â€˘ `Grade` -> `Grade...3` #> â€˘ `score` -> `score...4` #> â€˘ `freq` -> `freq...5` #> â€˘ `Grade` -> `Grade...6`    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 Ă— 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> # â„ą 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 Ă— 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # â„ą 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 Ă— 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level1 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level2 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2 #> # â„ą 2,245 more rows #>  #> $item_data #> # A tibble: 90 Ă— 6 #>   GCA   Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50 #>   <chr> <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl> #> 1 M1    M1_1               1     1                   1     102. #> 2 M1    M1_2               1     1                   2     107. #> 3 M1    M1_3               1     1                   3     110. #> 4 M1    M1_4               1     1                   4     113. #> 5 M1    M1_5               1     1                   5     116. #> # â„ą 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 226 Ă— 9 #>   score...1 freq...2 Grade...3 score...4 freq...5 Grade...6 score...7 freq...8 #>       <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> #> 1        86        6 M1              113       10 M2              126        8 #> 2        87        6 M1              114        2 M2              127        7 #> 3        88        2 M1              115        3 M2              128        5 #> 4        89        4 M1              116        4 M2              129        5 #> 5        90        6 M1              117        7 M2              130        6 #> # â„ą 221 more rows #> # â„ą 1 more variable: Grade...9 <chr>  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                tests = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

title: specifying-manual-cut-scores
code: library(EmStanS) set.seed(100) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300) #> New names: #> New names: #> New names: #> New names: #> New names: #> New names: #> New names: #> â€˘ `score` -> `score...1` #> â€˘ `freq` -> `freq...2` #> â€˘ `Grade` -> `Grade...3` #> â€˘ `score` -> `score...4` #> â€˘ `freq` -> `freq...5` #> â€˘ `Grade` -> `Grade...6`    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 Ă— 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2â€¦ #> # â„ą 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 Ă— 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # â„ą 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 Ă— 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level2 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level3 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2 #> # â„ą 2,245 more rows #>  #> $item_data #> # A tibble: 90 Ă— 6 #>   GCA   Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50 #>   <chr> <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl> #> 1 M1    M1_1               1     1                   1     111. #> 2 M1    M1_2               1     1                   2     134. #> 3 M1    M1_3               1     1                   3     134. #> 4 M1    M1_4               1     1                   4     141. #> 5 M1    M1_5               1     1                   5     152. #> # â„ą 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 227 Ă— 9 #>   score...1 freq...2 Grade...3 score...4 freq...5 Grade...6 score...7 freq...8 #>       <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> #> 1       100        3 M1              111        9 M2              119        6 #> 2       101        3 M1              112        4 M2              120        7 #> 3       102        4 M1              113        8 M2              121        5 #> 4       103        5 M1              114        4 M2              122        8 #> 5       104        3 M1              115        3 M2              123        3 #> # â„ą 222 more rows #> # â„ą 1 more variable: Grade...9 <chr>  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                tests = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

Embedded Standard Setting â€” EmStanS-package
text: Embedded Standard Setting (ESS) approach establish performance standards augments embedded within existing principled assessment design (PAD) practices. ESS, achievement level descriptor (ALD) writing alignment test items specified achievement levels viewed fundamental standard-setting activities cut scores estimated optimizing relationship item-ALD alignments empirically. ESS, package provides cut score estimation algorithm (referred ESS-Count ESS-Weight).

what: Get predictions from a GAM model. â€” predict_gam
title: Get predictions from a GAM model. â€” predict_gam
text: `r lifecycle::badge("superseded")` function superseded package tidymv. Please, use tidygam package instead. returns tibble predictions terms [gam][mgcv::gam] [bam][mgcv::bam] model. simply want return tibble predicted values response/outcome variable based terms (minus excluded smooth terms), set `type = "link"` (default). Note `type = "link"`, parametric terms excluded prediction, due limitations `mgcv`. want return tibble predicted values response/outcome variable term model separately, set `type = "terms"`. type can helpful want flexibility plotting.

title: Get predictions from a GAM model. â€” predict_gam
code: predict_gam(   model,   exclude_terms = NULL,   length_out = 50,   values = NULL,   type = "link" )

title: Get predictions from a GAM model. â€” predict_gam
text: model `gam` `bam` model object. exclude_terms Terms excluded prediction. Term names given appear model summary (example, `"s(x0,x1)"`). length_out integer indicating many values along numeric predictors use predicting outcome term (default `50`). values User supplied values specific terms named list. value `NULL`, first value term selected (useful excluding terms). type Either `"link"` `"terms"`. See Details .

title: Get predictions from a GAM model. â€” predict_gam
Get predictions from a GAM model. â€” predict_gam
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(mgcv) set.seed(10) data <- gamSim(4) model <- gam(y ~ fac + s(x2) + s(x2, by = fac) + s(x0), data = data)  # get predictions p <- predict_gam(model)  # get predictions excluding x0 (the coefficient of x0 is set to 0); # setting the value for the excluded term to NULL with the argument 'values' # reduces computation time p_2 <- predict_gam(model, exclude_terms = "s(x0)", values = list(x0 = NULL))  # get predictions with chosen values of x0  p_3 <- predict_gam(model, values = list(x0 = c(0.250599, 0.503313, 0.756028))) } # }
